- For multiple_choice: user taps option → autoflip happens which captures state and renders back
- For gap_fill: user presses Enter → autoflip happens 
- For audio_listening: user presses Enter → autoflip happens
- For usage_cases/deep_dive/formula_table/image_description: user taps anywhere → flip, always shows back (they always have answered=true implicitly via the else branch)

So for the 5 patterns (usage_cases, deep_dive, formula_table, image_description, audio_listening): they need quizState.answered to be true to render the back. But for usage_cases/deep_dive/formula_table/image_description, quizState.answered is never set to true in handleFlip... So would the back never show?

Actually wait - looking at `handleAutoFlip` (line 808-821), it just renders the back immediately without checking answered state. And for MCQ, options clicking triggers handleAutoFlip. For gap_fill, Enter triggers handleAutoFlip.

For patterns without interactive elements (usage_cases, deep_dive, formula_table, image_description), the user just taps the card, which calls handleFlip. if (backDiv && quizState.answered) {

For usage_cases/deep_dive/formula_table/image_description: if not answered, the condition at line 801 is false (these patterns ARE in the exclude list), so the back HTML is NOT updated. But the `cardBack` div already has HTML from `renderStudyScreen()` which calls `renderCardBack(card, currentQuizState)` at line 872. So the back already has content from initial render! For multiple_choice/gap_fill: these are NOT in the exclude list, so even without answering, the back renders. But for these types, the user is expected to answer first, and handleAutoFlip renders the back with the result.

### Database schema for pattern_type

The pattern is stored as an ENUM column in the `cards` table. From `Card.php` line 136-146:
```php
SHOW COLUMNS FROM cards WHERE Field = 'pattern_type'
```
The ENUM contains all 7 types, default value is 'usage_cases'.

The `ensurePatternTypeEnum` method dynamically alters the table to add new types to the ENUM if they're not already present.

### Admin filter styles (mapping):
- `usage_cases` → `text`
- `deep_dive` → `text`
- `formula_table` → `text`
- `multiple_choice` → `mcq`
- `gap_fill` → `gap`
- `image_description` → `image`
- `audio_listening` → `audio`

This is defined in `getStyleClass()` in admin.js (lines 91-97) and used for filtering and CSS class assignment.

---

## Complete Card Pattern Types Report

### Pattern Type Definitions

The system defines **7 card pattern types**, declared in `Card.php` (line 133) and used consistently across the entire codebase:

| # | Pattern Type | Icon | Category | Admin Label |
|---|-------------|------|----------|-------------|
| 1 | `usage_cases` | 📘 | Pure Text | Usage Cases |
| 2 | `deep_dive` | 🧠 | Pure Text | Deep Dive |
| 3 | `formula_table` | 📐 | Pure Text | Formula Table |
| 4 | `multiple_choice` | ❓ | Quiz | Multiple Choice |
| 5 | `gap_fill` | ✏️ | Quiz | Gap Fill |
| 6 | `image_description` | 🖼️ | Media | Image Description |
| 7 | `audio_listening` | 🎧 | Media | Audio Listening |

**Default**: `usage_cases` (set in `Card.php` line 101 and `admin.js` line 545)

---

### Database Schema for `pattern_type`

- **Table**: `cards`
- **Column**: `pattern_type`
- **Type**: `ENUM('usage_cases','deep_dive','formula_table','multiple_choice','gap_fill','image_description','audio_listening')`
- **Default**: `'usage_cases'`
- **Auto-maintained**: `Card::ensurePatternTypeEnum()` (`Card.php` lines 131-146) dynamically `ALTER TABLE`s the ENUM to add any missing values if a new pattern type is introduced.

---

### Admin Filter Style Mapping

In `admin.js` (`getStyleClass()`, lines 91-97), pattern types are grouped into 5 filter styles:

| Filter Style | Pattern Types |
|-------------|---------------|
| `text` | `usage_cases`, `deep_dive`, `formula_table` |
| `mcq` | `multiple_choice` |
| `gap` | `gap_fill` |
| `image` | `image_description` |
| `audio` | `audio_listening` |

**CSS classes for the badge in admin card list** (`admin.css` lines 145-149):
```css
.card-type.text   { background: #dbeafe; color: #1e40af; }  /* blue */
.card-type.mcq    { background: #fef3c7; color: #92400e; }  /* amber */
.card-type.gap    { background: #dcfce7; color: #166534; }  /* green */
.card-type.image  { background: #f3e8ff; color: #6b21a8; }  /* purple */
.card-type.audio  { background: #fce7f3; color: #9d174d; }  /* pink */
```

---

### 1. `usage_cases` — Pure Text / Usage Cases

**Front render** (app.js lines 649-656, the `else` branch):
```
<div class="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full min-h-[200px]">
    <h1 class="text-xl md:text-2xl md:text-3xl text-center font-bold marker-underline">TITLE</h1>
    <p class="text-sm text-gray-400 mt-3">👆 Tap card to flip</p>
</div>
```
- Shows only the card title (formatted with `\br`, `\b`, `\i`, `\u`, `\em`, `\strong` tags).
- No interactive elements.

**Back render** (app.js lines 750-770, the `else` branch):
```
<h3>title</h3>
<div class="card-definition bg-blue-50 ..."> definition (data.definition ?? data.usage1) </div>
<div> Example: data.example1a ?? data.example </div>
```
- Shows title, definition, and optional example.
- Blue background box for definition.

**Admin editor fields** (admin.js lines 283-293, `else` branch):
- **Definition / Description** (`textarea`): stored as `content_data.definition`. Pre-filled from `data.definition || data.usage1`.
- **Example** (`textarea`): stored as `content_data.example`. Pre-filled from `data.example1a || data.example`.

**CSV import** (`api/import_csv.php` lines 172-180 & 182-184):
- Reads `definition`, `usage1`, `example1`-`example4`, `tip`.
- Stores `definition`, `usage1`, `example1a`, `tip`, `example` in `content_data`.
- If `usage1` is empty but examples exist, uses first example as `usage1`.

---

### 2. `deep_dive` — Pure Text / Deep Dive

- **Front render**: **Identical** to `usage_cases` (falls into same `else` branch). Shows title only.
- **Back render**: **Identical** to `usage_cases` (falls into same `else` branch). Shows definition + example.
- **Admin editor**: **Identical** to `usage_cases` (falls into same `else` branch).
- **CSV import**: Same pure-text handling as `usage_cases`.
- **CSV column docs**: `definition`, `example1`-`example4`, `tip`.

---

### 3. `formula_table` — Pure Text / Formula Table

- **Front render**: **Identical** to `usage_cases` (same `else` branch). Shows title only.
- **Back render**: **Identical** to `usage_cases` (same `else` branch). Shows definition + example.
- **Admin editor**: **Identical** to `usage_cases` (same `else` branch).
- **CSV import**: Same pure-text handling.
- **CSV column docs**: `definition`, `example1`-`example4`, `tip`.

**Note**: These three "Pure Text" types are semantically distinct (usage context, deep explanation, formula/structure) but **render identically** in both front-end and admin. The only difference is the label shown in the editor selectors.

---

### 4. `multiple_choice` — Quiz / Multiple Choice

**Front render** (app.js lines 600-611):
```
<div class="text-center">
    <p>❓ question_text</p>
    <div class="grid/space-y-3">
        <div class="quiz-option" data-idx="0">A. Option A</div>
        <div class="quiz-option" data-idx="1">B. Option B</div>
        ...
    </div>
    <p>👆 Tap your answer, then flip the card</p>
</div>
```
- Grid layout for 4+ options, stacked for 3 or fewer (`grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-3` vs `space-y-3`).
- Options are clickable, get `.selected` class (blue highlight `#dbeafe` / `#1d4ed8`).
- Clicking an option triggers `handleAutoFlip` after 400ms.

**Back render** (app.js lines 664-688):
```
<h3>✓ Answer</h3>
<div class="bg-green-50 ..."> Correct option letter + text </div>
<div class="correct/incorrect bg"> ✅ Correct! / ❌ Incorrect. </div>
<div> 📝 explanation </div>
```
- Green box with correct answer.
- If user selected, shows green/red feedback.
- Optional explanation.

**Admin editor fields** (admin.js lines 199-222):
- **Question Text** (`input`)
- **Options** (`input`, comma-separated string, parsed to array)
- **Correct Index** (`number`, 0-based)
- **Explanation** (`textarea`, supports formatting tags)

**Import editor fields** (admin.js lines 1728-1733, MCQ fields lines 581-592):
- Definition label → "Question Text"
- Extra label → "Explanation"
- Plus 4 option inputs + correct index.

**CSV import** (`api/import_csv.php` lines 123-143):
- Reads `question_text`, `opt1`-`opt4`, `correct_answer` (0-based index), `explanation`.
- Stores `options` (array), `correct_index`, `question_text`, `explanation`.

---

### 5. `gap_fill` — Quiz / Gap Fill

**Front render** (app.js lines 612-623):
```
<div class="text-center">
    <p>✏️ Complete the sentence:</p>
    <p class="bg-gray-100 ..."> sentence with blank </p>
    <div class="context-hint"> Auto-generated hint </div>
    <input type="text" id="gapFillInput" placeholder="Type your answer...">
    <p>👆 Type answer, then flip to check</p>
</div>
```
- Shows sentence (with `______` indicating blank).
- **Context hint** is auto-generated by `getGapFillHint()` (app.js lines 313-343) based on card title/sentence keywords (past, present perfect, modal, future, continuous, passive, conditional).
- Text input; pressing Enter triggers `handleAutoFlip`.

**Back render** (app.js lines 689-711):
```
<h3>✓ Correct Answer</h3>
<div class="bg-green-50 ..."> correct_answers.join(' / ') </div>
<div> ✅ Correct! / ❌ Incorrect. Your answer: "..." </div>
<div> 📝 Example: example </div>
```
- Shows all acceptable answers separated by ` / `.
- User's answer checked with case-insensitive match.
- Optional example.

**Admin editor fields** (admin.js lines 223-240):
- **Sentence with blank** (`textarea`)
- **Correct Answer(s)** (`input`, comma-separated)
- **Example Sentence** (`textarea`)

**Import editor fields** (admin.js lines 1734-1738):
- Definition label → "Sentence"
- Extra label → "Correct Answers"

**CSV import** (`api/import_csv.php` lines 144-150):
- Reads `sentence`, `correct_answer` (comma-separated for multiple), `example1`.
- Stores `sentence`, `correct_answers` (array), `example`.

---

### 6. `image_description` — Media / Image

**Front render** (app.js lines 624-633):
```
<div class="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full min-h-[200px]">
    <h1>🖼️ title</h1>
    <img> or <div class="text-6xl">🖼️</div> (fallback)
    <p>👆 Tap card to flip</p>
</div>
```
- Shows title, image (if valid URL: `http://`, `https://`, or `uploads/`), otherwise large emoji icon.
- No interactive elements.

**Back render** (app.js lines 712-724):
```
<h3>title</h3>
<div class="card-definition bg-blue-50 ..."> description </div>
```
- Shows title and description in blue box.

**Admin editor fields** (admin.js lines 241-256):
- **Image URL** (`url input` + upload button for JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP)
- **Description** (`textarea`, shown on back)

**Import editor fields** (admin.js lines 1739-1743):
- Definition label → "Image URL"
- Extra label → "Description"

**CSV import** (`api/import_csv.php` lines 151-157):
- Reads `image_url`, `description`.
- Stores `image_url`, `description`.

---

### 7. `audio_listening` — Media / Audio

**Front render** (app.js lines 634-648):
```
<div class="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full min-h-[200px]">
    <h1>🎧 title</h1>
    <audio controls> or <div class="text-6xl">🎧</div> (fallback)
    <p> prompt (if present) </p>
    <input type="text"> (if interactive: has prompt or correct_answers)
    <p>👆 Tap card to flip</p>
</div>
```
- Shows title, audio player controls (if valid URL), optional prompt.
- **Interactive mode**: If `prompt` or `correct_answers` are present, shows text input.
- **Transcription mode**: If `transcript` exists but no `correct_answers`, input placeholder says "Type what you hear..."

**Back render** (app.js lines 725-749):
```
<h3>title</h3>
<div class="bg-green-50 ..."> transcript </div>
<div> ✅ Correct! / ❌ Your answer: "..." </div>
```
- Shows transcript/notes.
- User's answer checked against `correct_answers` (if present) or against exact `transcript` match.
- If no answer expected, shows "Passive listening".

**Admin editor fields** (admin.js lines 257-282):
- **Audio URL** (`url input` + upload button for MP3/WAV/OGG)
- **Prompt / Question** (`textarea`, optional — if filled, user types an answer)
- **Correct Answer(s)** (`input`, comma-separated, optional — if empty uses transcription mode)
- **Transcript / Notes** (`textarea`, shown on back)

**Import editor fields** (admin.js lines 1744-1748):
- Definition label → "Audio URL"
- Extra label → "Transcript / Answer"

**CSV import** (`api/import_csv.php` lines 158-170):
- Reads `audio_url`, `prompt`, `transcript`, `correct_answer`.
- Stores `audio_url`, `prompt`, `transcript`, optionally `correct_answers` array.

---

### Flip/Interaction Behavior Summary

From `handleFlip()` (app.js lines 773-806) and `handleAutoFlip()` (app.js lines 808-821):

| Pattern Type | Interactive? | Answer Capture | Auto-Flip on Interaction |
|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------------------------|
| `usage_cases` | No | None | No (tap to flip) |
| `deep_dive` | No | None | No (tap to flip) |
| `formula_table` | No | None | No (tap to flip) |
| `multiple_choice` | Yes | Captures `selectedIdx` from `.quiz-option.selected` | Yes, 400ms after option click |
| `gap_fill` | Yes | Captures `userAnswer` from `#gapFillInput` | Yes, on Enter key |
| `image_description` | No | None | No (tap to flip) |
| `audio_listening` | Yes (if prompt/answers exist), No (passive) | Captures `userAnswer` from `#gapFillInput` | Yes, on Enter key (interactive mode) |

For non-interactive patterns, the back is pre-rendered in `renderStudyScreen()` (line 872) so flipping just reveals it. For interactive patterns, the back is re-rendered with answer feedback upon flip.

---

### Admin Pattern Type Selectors

Both the card editor (`admin_cards.php` lines 345-352) and the import editor (`admin.php`/`admin.js` import section) use the same option list:

```html
<option value="usage_cases">📘 Pure Text - Usage Cases</option>
<option value="deep_dive">🧠 Pure Text - Deep Dive</option>
<option value="formula_table">📐 Pure Text - Formula Table</option>
<option value="multiple_choice">❓ Multiple Choice</option>
<option value="gap_fill">✏️ Gap Fill</option>
<option value="image_description">🖼️ Image Description</option>
<option value="audio_listening">🎧 Audio Listening</option>
```

  Line 10: ,Spanish Verbs: Present Tense,multiple_choice,Modal Probability - 95% Certainty,Intermediate,Choose the correct modal for 95% certainty: It _____ rain tomorrow.,,will,must,should,could,1,"'Must' expresses strong logical deduction (about 95% certainty) when you have evidence. It must have rained.","'Must' = logical conclusion (95%).","Use 'must' for strong deduction"

  Line 11: ,Spanish Verbs: Present Tense,multiple_choice,Modal Probability - 100% Certainty,Intermediate,Choose the correct modal for 100% certainty: It _____ rain tomorrow.,,will,must,should,could,0,"'Will' expresses a prediction with 100% certainty about the future.   Line 12: ,Spanish Verbs: Present Tense,multiple_choice,Modal Probability - 50% Certainty,Intermediate,Choose the correct modal for 50% certainty: It _____ rain tomorrow.,,will,must,should,could,3,"'Could' expresses possibility (around 50%). It means it's possible but not certain. The other modals express higher certainty.","It could snow tonight, but it's not guaranteed.","She could come to the party, or maybe not.","'Could' = 50% possibility.","Use 'could' for possibility (50%)"

  Line 13: ,Spanish Verbs: Present Tense,multiple_choice,Modal Probability - 30% Certainty,Intermediate,Choose the correct modal for 30% certainty: It _____ rain tomorrow.,,will,must,should,might,0,"'Might' expresses weaker possibility (around 30-40%).   Line 14: ,Spanish Verbs: Present Tense,multiple_choice,Modal Probability - 60% Certainty,Intermediate,Choose the correct modal for 60% certainty: It _____ rain tomorrow.,,will,must,should,may,2,"'Should' expresses expected probability (60-80%). 595:     function renderCardFront(card) {
596:         const pattern = card.pattern_type;
597:         const data = card.content_data || {};
598:         const title = card.title || 'Flashcard';
599: 
600:         if (pattern === 'multiple_choice') {
601:             const options = data.options || ['Option A', 'Option B', 'Option C'];
602:             const gridClass = options.length > 3 ? 'grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-3' : 'space-y-3';
603:             return `
604:                 <div class="text-center">
605:                     <p class="text-lg mb-3 font-bold">❓ ${formatBreaks(escapeHtml(card.question_text || 'Select the correct answer:'))}</p>
606:                     <div class="${gridClass}" id="mcqOptionsContainer">
607:                         ${options.map((opt, idx) => `<div class="quiz-option" data-idx="${idx}">${String.fromCharCode(65+idx)}. ${formatBreaks(escapeHtml(opt))}</div>`).join('')}
608:                     </div>
609:                     <p class="text-sm text-gray-400 mt-2">👆 Tap your answer, then flip the card</p>
610:                 </div>
611:             `;
612:         } else if (pattern === 'gap_fill') {
613:             const sentence = data.sentence || 'Complete the sentence: ______';
614:             const hint = getGapFillHint(card);
615:             return `
616:                 <div class="text-center">
617:                     <p class="text-lg mb-1 font-bold">✏️ Complete the sentence:</p>
618:                     <p class="text-sm bg-gray-100 p-3 rounded-xl mb-1">${formatBreaks(escapeHtml(sentence))}</p>
619:                     <div class="context-hint">${hint}</div>
620:                     <input type="text" id="gapFillInput" placeholder="Type your answer..." class="w-full p-2 text-sm border-2 rounded-xl mb-2" autocomplete="off">
621:                     <p class="text-sm text-gray-400 mt-1">👆 Type answer, then flip to check</p>
622:                 </div>
623:             `;
624:         } else if (pattern === 'image_mcq') {
625:             const imageUrl = data.image_url || '';
626:             const hasImage = imageUrl && (imageUrl.startsWith('http://') || imageUrl.startsWith('https://') || imageUrl.startsWith('uploads/'));
627:             const options = data.options || ['Option A', 'Option B', 'Option C'];
628:             return `
629:                 <div class="flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-3 md:gap-4 h-full min-h-[200px]">
630:                     <div class="flex items-center justify-center md:w-1/2 bg-gray-50 rounded-xl p-2">
631:                         ${hasImage
632:                             ? `<img src="${escapeHtml(imageUrl)}" alt="${escapeHtml(title)}" class="max-h-48 md:max-h-full w-full object-contain rounded-lg" onerror="this.style.display='none'">`
633:                             : `<div class="text-6xl text-gray-300">🖼️</div>`
634:                         }
635:                     </div>
636:                     <div class="flex flex-col justify-center md:w-1/2 gap-2" id="mcqOptionsContainer">
637:                         <p class="text-sm font-bold text-center md:text-left mb-1">Select the correct answer:</p>
638:                         ${options.map((opt, idx) => `<div class="quiz-option text-sm md:text-base py-2 px-3" data-idx="${idx}">${String.fromCharCode(65+idx)}. ${formatBreaks(escapeHtml(opt))}</div>`).join('')}
639:                         <p class="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-1 text-center">👆 Tap your answer, then flip</p>

678:     function renderCardBack(card, quizState) {
679:         const data = card.content_data || {};
680:         const pattern = card.pattern_type;
681:         const title = card.title || 'Flashcard';
682: 
683:         if (pattern === 'multiple_choice') {
684:             const options = data.options || ['Option A', 'Option B', 'Option C'];
685:             const correctIdx = data.correct_index !== undefined ? data.correct_index : 1;
686:             const correctAnswer = options[correctIdx];
687:             const selectedIdx = quizState?.selectedIdx;
688:             const isCorrect = (selectedIdx === correctIdx);
689:             const explanation = formatBreaks(escapeHtml(data.explanation || ''));
690: 
691:             return `
692:                 <div class="back-content">

695:                         <div class="bg-green-50 p-3 rounded-xl border-2 border-green-300 mb-2">
696:                             <p class="text-base font-bold">${String.fromCharCode(65+correctIdx)}. ${formatBreaks(escapeHtml(correctAnswer))}</p>
697:                         </div>

23:         .error { color: #dc2626; background: #fee2e2; padding: 8px 12px; margin: 4px 0; border-left: 4px solid #dc2626; }
24:         .info { color: #2563eb; background: #dbeafe; padding: 8px 12px; margin: 4px 0; border-left: 4px solid #2563eb; }
25:         .warning { color: #d97706; background: #fef3c7; padding: 8px 12px; margin: 4px 0; border-left: 4px solid #d97706; }
26:         h1 { color: #1e293b; }
27:         hr { margin: 20px 0; }
28:     </style>
29: </head>
30: <body>
31: <div class="container">
32:     <h1>📥 CSV Importer for Flashcard System</h1>
33:     <hr>
34: ';
35: 
36: function display($msg, $type = 'info') {
37:     echo "<div class='$type'>$msg</div>";
38:     flush();
39: }
40: 
41: try {
42:     $pdo = Database::getConnection();
43:     display("✅ Connected to database", 'success');
44: } catch (PDOException $e) {
45:     display("❌ Connection failed: " . $e->getMessage(), 'error');
46:     exit;
47: }
48: 
49: // ============ IMPORT CARD SETS ============
50: display("📚 Importing card sets from: card_sets.csv", 'info');
51: 
52: if (!file_exists($setsFile)) {
53:     display("❌ File not found: card_sets.csv", 'error');
54: } else {
55:     $handle = fopen($setsFile, 'r');
56:     if ($handle === false) {
57:         display("❌ Could not open file: card_sets.csv", 'error');
58:     } else {
59:         $header = fgetcsv($handle, 0, ',', '"', '\\');
60:         display("Header columns: " . implode(', ', $header), 'info');
61: 
62:         $inserted = 0;
63:         $skipped = 0;
64: 
65:         while (($row = fgetcsv($handle, 0, ',', '"', '\\')) !== false) {
66:             if (count($row) < 3) continue;
67: 
68:             $id = $row[0];
69:             $name = $row[1];
70:             $description = $row[2] ?? '';
71: 
72:             $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT id FROM card_sets WHERE id = ? OR name = ?");
73:             $stmt->execute([$id, $name]);
74:             if ($stmt->fetch()) {
75:                 display("⏭️ Skipped (exists): ID $id - $name", 'warning');
76:                 $skipped++;
77:                 continue;
78:             }
79: 
80:             try {
81:                 $stmt = $pdo->prepare("INSERT INTO card_sets (id, name, description) VALUES (?, ?, ?)");
82:                 $stmt->execute([$id, $name, $description]);
83:                 display("✅ Inserted: ID $id - $name", 'success');
84:                 $inserted++;
85:             } catch (PDOException $e) {
86:                 display("❌ Error: " . $e->getMessage(), 'error');
87:             }
88:         }
89:         fclose($handle);
90:         display("📊 Card sets: $inserted inserted, $skipped skipped", 'info');
91:     }
92: }
93: 
94: // ============ IMPORT CARDS ============
95: display("<hr>📇 Importing cards from: cards.csv", 'info');
96: 
97: if (!file_exists($cardsFile)) {
98:     display("❌ File not found: cards.csv", 'error');
99: } else {
100:     $handle = fopen($cardsFile, 'r');
101:     if ($handle === false) {
102:         display("❌ Could not open file: cards.csv", 'error');
103:     } else {
104:         $header = fgetcsv($handle, 0, ',', '"', '\\');
105:         display("Header columns: " . implode(', ', $header), 'info');
106: 
107:         $inserted = 0;
108:         $skipped = 0;
109:         $errors = 0;
110:         $lineNum = 1;
111: 
112:         while (($row = fgetcsv($handle, 0, ',', '"', '\\')) !== false) {
113:             $lineNum++;
114:             if (count($row) < 6) {
115:                 display("⚠️ Line $lineNum: Skipped (only " . count($row) . " columns)", 'warning');
116:                 $skipped++;
117:                 continue;
118:             }
119: 
120:             $set_id = $row[0];
121:             $title = $row[1];
122:             $pattern_type = $row[2];
123:             $level = $row[3];
124:             $question_text = $row[4];
125:             $content_data = $row[5];
126: 
127:             if (!json_decode($content_data)) {
128:                 $content_data = str_replace("'", '"', $content_data);
129:                 if (!json_decode($content_data)) {
130:                     display("⚠️ Line $lineNum: Invalid JSON for '{$title}'", 'warning');
131:                     $errors++;
132:                     continue;
133:                 }
134:             }
135: 
136:             $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT id FROM cards WHERE set_id = ? AND title = ?");
137:             $stmt->execute([$set_id, $title]);
138:             if ($stmt->fetch()) {
139:                 $skipped++;
140:                 continue;
141:             }
142: 
143:             try {
144:                 $stmt = $pdo->prepare("INSERT INTO cards (set_id, title, pattern_type, level, question_text, content_data) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)");
145:                 $stmt->execute([$set_id, $title, $pattern_type, $level, $question_text, $content_data]);
146:                 $inserted++;
147:                 if ($inserted % 100 == 0) {
148:                     display("📈 Progress: $inserted cards inserted...", 'info');
149:                 }
150:             } catch (PDOException $e) {
151:                 display("❌ Error on line $lineNum: " . $e->getMessage(), 'error');
152:                 $errors++;
153:             }
154:         }
155:         fclose($handle);
156:         display("<hr>📊 Final results: $inserted inserted, $skipped skipped, $errors errors", 'info');
157:     }
158: }
159: 
160: // ============ VERIFICATION ============
161: display("<hr>🔍 Verification", 'info');
162: $setCount = $pdo->query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM card_sets")->fetchColumn();
163: $cardCount = $pdo->query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cards")->fetchColumn();
164: display("📊 Database now has: $setCount card sets and $cardCount cards", 'success');
165: 
166: echo '</div></body></html>';

(End of file - total 166 lines)
</content>

651:                     <p class="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-2">👆 Type answer, then flip</p>

- **`image_mcq`** **NOT handled** — falls through to `else` at line 996, setting `wasCorrect = true` **always**, regardless of the user's selected answer
